signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Bias
signals.text: NZD/CAD G8 spread at 1.206% marks a critical bilateral divergence. NZD outperformance is driven by the RBNZ hike bias and (2.25%). CAD remains the G8 laggard as oil price volatility and a bear steepener regime in US yields weigh on the growth-sensitive loonie. NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 vs RBNZ hike bias suggests a short-squeeze is fueling the move. Watch for the RBNZ meeting on 27 May to confirm the hawkish trajectory.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.84 remains bid as the +300bp Fed-BoJ carry differential dominates despite US-Iran military exchanges. While VIX at 17.1 indicates normal volatility, repeated May holiday interventions by Japan and a 160.00 watch level for verbal warnings create a ceiling for the pair. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 shows crowded carry positioning vulnerable to a geopolitical escalation if VIX crosses 25. Watch for BoJ June hike signals to compress the spread.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7226 exhibits a sharp divergence between institutional and retail flows. COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) indicates heavy institutional backing, while retail sentiment is 83% short. This extreme retail fade of the institutional trend suggests a continuation of the AUD bid as retail shorts are forced to cover. The +60bp AUD carry premium over the Fed supports the move. Watch for a break of 0.7300 to trigger retail capitulation.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Trade Risk
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.715 vs a norm of -0.141 (z=+1.92σ) signals a regime shift where EUR is tracking equity sentiment rather than traditional macro drivers. Trump's July 4 deadline for the Turnberry deal introduces structural downside risk via tariff escalation, which would reduce EU export competitiveness. Despite peace optimism in the Middle East supporting 1.1740, the +175bp Fed-ECB carry advantage structurally limits upside. Watch US NFP data for the next USD catalyst.
